name: Oregon Physical Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Oregon Physical Therapist Licensing Board
800 NE Oregon Street, Suite 407
Portland, OR 97232-2187

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Oregon physical therapist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $195. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.
Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Oregon have reciprocity for physical therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Oregon participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Oregon without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Oregon?

acceptedAnswer.text: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
